parsing_approach: Comment/string-aware masking pass (state-machine lexer), then structural matching with brace-matched spans and brace-depth checks. Not a full grammar parser, but immune to the classic regex failures: declarations inside comments or string literals, spans cut short by nested braces, anonymous-class methods attributed to the enclosing type.
extracts: class/interface/enum declarations incl. nested types, extends/implements, annotations
methods and constructors with line spans, signatures, modifiers, annotations, overloads
known_limitations: Not a full Java grammar: no expression-level analysis, no symbol resolution (extends/implements names are unresolved strings).
Java 15+ text blocks (""") are not handled by the lexer.
Records, sealed types, and annotation type declarations (@interface) are not extracted.
Enum constants and fields are not extracted as nodes.
Complex generic return types with nested angle brackets may cause a method to be skipped (skipped, never mis-parsed).
